source: Elevated red cell distribution width (RDW) is common in SLE due to increased red cell production variability.
Anemia is a frequent finding in SLE, often due to chronic inflammation and autoimmune hemolysis.

Leukocytosis can occur in SLE due to inflammation and immune complex deposition.
Thrombocytopenia is common in SLE, resulting from autoimmune destruction of platelets.
Macrocytosis can be seen in SLE, particularly in cases with drug-induced effects or vitamin B12 deficiency.
